description:

(It's free and international!)

Scoring system:

How the score is calculated: 

1. This test is split into many different categories, for example “Ultrawide video quality” or “low-light zoom photography”
2. Each category is assigned an importance rating out of 10, for example, “daylight photography”, which gets a 10 is far more important for most people than “5x Zoom video in Low-light”, which gets a 2
3. Within each category, each phone gets a score out of 10 for how good it is in that category. 
4. The number of points a phone gets in each category = Importance of category x Score in that category.  For example, if the Huawei P30 Pro scored 5/10 in a catgory that’s 5/10 important, then The P30 Pro’s score for this category would be 5 x 5 = 25 points
5. (Some categories may change between camera comparisons as new features are introduced, so scores cannot be compared BETWEEN tests, only WITHIN them)

title: OnePlus 6T vs iPhone XR / XS vs Mate 20 Pro vs Note 9 Battery Life DRAIN TEST
description:
Battery Life drain Test of the New 2018 OnePlus 6T, with the iPhone XS / iPhone XS Max, Samsung Galaxy Note 9,  and Huawei Mate 20 Pro!  This Battery Life Drain Test will show us which smartphone has the best battery life!  

FAQ:

1. Yes I know all phones are not on max brightness.  I talk about this in the video, and it's to compensate for the varying brightness of the screens - I have set them all to an EQUAL brightness level, not each phone's MAXIMUM brightness.

2.  No the iPhones are not screen-recording.  That red thing surrounding the timer is Auto-Shazam, which I turned on for all phones.

3.  The iPhone XR is not performing close to what Apple (and some other tests) claim, and my suspicion is, as I mention in the video, that these test were performed with all phones at max brightness, whereas, because the XR's screen is not as bright / viewing angles not as good, in this video I have had to set it's brightness percentage higher to compensate - In my opinion this is the fairest way to do it.  The result we got here agrees with some other XR battery comparisons on YouTube.

4.  Some of you are commenting that in past battery tests, the iPhone XS Max has beaten the Note 9 - this is because in those tests, the Note 9 was set at QHD+ resolution, but in this one, at FHD+.
